title: Cooking skills - Canada.ca
content:
Showing 9 out of 16 cooking skills
10 reasons to buy frozen vegetables and fruits
Frozen vegetables and fruits are a nutritious, tasty and efficient option, available year round. They work well in many recipes! Not convinced yet? Read our article.
7 homemade seasoning mixes
You can enhance the flavour of food by seasoning your meals with herbs and spices. Store-bought spice mixes can be costly and often contain a lot of sodium. Create your own spice mix recipes using simple ingredients.
Adjusting recipes to meet your needs
Knowing how to adjust recipes in a way that works for you is an important skill to help you eat healthily.
Best uses for fresh and dried herbs
Herbs are an excellent way to add flavour to your meals without adding extra sodium, sugars, or saturated fat. Learn how to use fresh and dried herbs.
Cooking with plant-based protein foods
Cooking with plant-based protein foods doesn’t need to be complicated! Follow these tips to learn how you can incorporate them in your meals and snacks.
Eight canned foods to keep on hand
Canned foods are often budget friendly and can be just as nutritious as fresh foods. They can help you prepare meals quickly and are good to keep on hand.
Five ingredients, five meals
You don't need a lot of ingredients to eat different meals! With a few household staples and a little imagination, you can make several recipes from the same foods.
How to blanch vegetables
Blanching is a food preparation technique that involves boiling vegetables for a short time, then submerging them in an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Meal planning from start to finish in 4 steps
Learn how meal planning simplifies making healthy meals in just 4 steps.
Meal planning using leftovers
Using leftover prepared ingredients is a great way to save time in the kitchen. It can also help you reduce food waste.
Measure ingredients like a pro
For recipes such as sauces and baked goods, it's best to measure ingredients accurately to ensure that the recipe is successful.
A simple guide to selecting fish
Fish is a protein food that contains important nutrients. Learn about different types of fish and how to prepare them.
Herbs are a great way to add flavour to your meals! Whether grown at home or bought at the store, there are lots of ways they can be stored.
Storing leafy greens
Unlike other fresh vegetables and fruits, leafy greens should be rinsed before going in the fridge. Follow these easy steps to store your leafy greens safely and extend their freshness.
Storing vegetables and fruits
Properly storing vegetables and fruits can help you cut down on food waste, get the most out of the food you buy and prevent foodborne illness.
What does it mean to season to taste?
You've probably come across the phrase "season to taste" when reading recipes. What does this phrase mean?
For recipes and more food guide resources visit: Canada.ca/FoodGuide
